title: GTM, Strategy & Delivery
key_evidence: Ashby API confirms this posting's ONLY location is 'New York' and isRemote=false. It is a US-market, in-person GTM/delivery hire. Ryan is UK-authorisation only and relocating to London, NOT the US — this is a near-blocker on work authorisation and physical location.

Domain is patent/IP/legaltech R&D tooling (analyses 150M patents, customers L'Oreal + law firm Vorys). Not Ryan's crypto/Web3 + AI-tooling domain. Bonus criterion is 'designing/delivering POCs to enterprise customers' — classic enterprise B2B delivery, which is explicitly NOT Ryan's profile (community-led growth, not enterprise delivery).

Requirements bar is generous on paper: '2+ years high-ownership environment (startups, consulting, banking)', builder's mindset, extreme ownership. Ryan's first-employee/founder's-associate background clears the experience bar comfortably.
20-person team, $24M+ raised (Atomico, Index, Norrsken). Small enough that a founder/hiring-manager likely reads applications — good screening signal.
Role explicitly warns: requires travel, no predefined playbook, high autonomy — suits a generalist operator like Ryan, but framed around enterprise POC delivery he hasn't done.
biggest_lever: Generalist high-ownership operator profile clears the stated 2+yr bar; founder-read likely on a 20-person team.
biggest_risk: New York in-person role with no UK/remote option = work-authorisation + location near-blocker for UK-only Ryan; domain (patent/IP legaltech) and enterprise-POC-delivery focus are both outside his profile.
apply_verdict: Skip unless willing/able to relocate to the US (visa). The DISTINCT London-based Founder's Associate role on Ankar's board is a far better fit; this NY GTM role is effectively gated by location.
